claude_reasoning: Acceptance rate has plateaued around 40-42% for 4 iterations with all metrics stable. Key observations: (1) Transient xcorr has a bimodal distribution (mean 0.72, p50 0.89) suggesting some samples have severely degraded transients, likely driving rejections. (2) Token change rate is low (~10%) yet acceptance is still poor, meaning the changes being made are sometimes too disruptive. (3) HF energy shows slight loss (-1.03 dB mean). Strategy: Fully eliminate attack masking (0.02→0.0) to maximally preserve transients. Increase k_candidates (24→28) for better candidate selection. Reduce temperature (0.45→0.35) and top_p (0.8→0.75) to make regenerated tokens more conservative—each changed token will be closer to the model's top predictions, reducing the chance of destructive edits. The tail mask stays the same so variation still comes from the tail region, but individual token replacements will be higher quality.
